articles.title: Pick Ekitike, Sesko and Van Dijk - FPL team of the week
articles.summary: It is a good week for Fantasy Premier League managers to load up on Liverpool and Manchester United players, write Thomas Woods.

articles.title: 'I'm ready' - Park bids to show Man Utd form for England
articles.summary: Manchester United midfielder Jess Park has been in brilliant form since her move in the summer - but can she take that into England's upcoming matches?

articles.title: Man Utd post profit but debt rises to £1.3bn
articles.summary: The amount Manchester United owe increases to almost £1.3bn in outstanding transfer fees, rolling credit facility and legacy debt.

articles.title: When is Champions League last-16 draw and how does it work?
articles.summary: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at when the Champions League knockout phase draw takes place and who Premier League teams could face.

articles.title: Bizarre VAR? Why Kelly's second yellow card became 'awful' straight red
articles.summary: An 'awful' decision, or 'always going to be a red'? Why Juventus' Lloyd Kelly's second yellow card became a straight red in his side's Champions League exit at the hands of Galatasaray.

articles.title: 'Darlings of Italian football' - how Atalanta salvaged national pride
articles.summary: As Italy faced the embarrassment of having no clubs in the Champions League last 16, Atalanta became the saviours of national reputation - and pride.

articles.title: 'He was always going to score' - Real beat Benfica to reach last 16
articles.summary: Watch as Real Madrid head through to the last 16 in the Champions League after a 2-1 victory against Benfica.

articles.title: Salah happy to stay at Liverpool - Thursday's gossip
articles.summary: Mohamed Salah would be happy to stay at Liverpool until 2027, there are six Premier League clubs interested in Frankfurt's Nnamdi Collins, while Casemiro wants to keep playing in Europe.

articles.title: PSG avoid late scare against 10-man Monaco
articles.summary: PSG held on despite a late Jordan Teze goal, after Marquinhos and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia scored in quick succesion to seal the tie for French champions, as Mamadou Coulibaly was sent off for Monaco.

articles.title: Reckless or unlucky? Did Juventus' Kelly deserve red card?
articles.summary: Watch as Steven Warnock, Rory Smith, and Nedum Onuoha analyse the events leading to Juventus' Lloyd Kelly receiving a red card on Match of the Day with Mark Chapman.

articles.title: Atalanta score dramatic late penalty to knock out Dortmund
articles.summary: Atalanta score a penalty with the last kick of the game, as the Italians stage an incredible fightback to beat Borussia Dortmund and progress to the last 16 of the Champions League.

articles.title: Brilliant Bodo/Glimt shock Inter to reach last 16
articles.summary: Bodo/Glimt produced one of the biggest upsets in Champions League history as they knocked out last year's finalists Inter Milan to reach the last 16 for the first time.

articles.title: Newcastle sweep aside Qarabag in Champions League
articles.summary: Newcastle progress to the Round of 16 in the Champions League, after comfortably beating Qarabag 3-2 (9-3 aggregate) in the knockout phase play-offs.

articles.title: Warnock questions Real Madrid's 'defensive mentality'
articles.summary: Watch as Steven Warnock, Rory Smith, and Nedum Onuoha break down how Real Madrid's defence are vulnerable, on Match of the Day with Mark Chapman.

articles.title: Man City, Atletico, now Inter - how Bodo/Glimt got to last 16
articles.summary: Watch Bodo/Glimt "tear up the European established order" as they make their way to the Champions League last 16, defeating giants like Manchester City, Atletico Madrid, and Inter Milan on the way.

articles.summary: Gianni Infantino has clashed with unions and European football, but after 10 years as Fifa president he remains in an impregnable position.
